summary: Complete the coherent C9 settings slice on the verified C6 parent: strict versioned per-user settings, immediate application with serialized durable rollback, bounded secret-free JSON exchange, Appendix C custom-theme import and live selection, browser session-first secret handling with explicit persisted opt-in, and desktop OS-keychain command wiring. Production credential-store smoke remains an explicit acceptance gap.
specSections: Section 2.7 questions, decisions, and unit of work
Section 4.1 FR-1.7 and FR-1.8 editor and keybinding settings
Section 4.4 FR-4.3 and FR-4.6 rendering and engine settings
Section 4.12 FR-12.1 through FR-12.5 custom-theme integration
Section 5 N-2, N-3, N-4, N-5, and N-6

externalInputs.purpose: Resolved the exact keyring 4.1.4 crate, confirmed its MIT OR Apache-2.0 metadata, and bound Cargo.lock to checksum f6ee8d4dae108d4177d0a0ce241f98acc1ef28e20837bdef43cff4d160cf70fe.
Used the public keyring Entry API contract for OS credential load, save, missing-entry, and delete behavior.

decisions.decision: Use one exact version-1 persisted profile for all nine settings sections, reject unknown, partial, oversized, or internally inconsistent data, cap custom themes at 32 unique entries, validate keybinding collisions, and exclude AI secret bytes from the schema and serializer by construction.
decisions.rationale: A single bounded authority makes restart and export/import behavior deterministic while making it impossible for ordinary settings persistence to serialize an AI key.

decisions.decision: Apply a validated profile optimistically, serialize all durable writes behind one promise tail, track the last durable profile, and roll back a failed write only when its optimistic profile is still the latest visible state.
decisions.rationale: FR-9.2 requires immediate application, while ordered writes and latest-only rollback prevent older asynchronous failures from overwriting newer accepted user intent.

decisions.decision: Use a dedicated browser local-storage key and one desktop platform-config JSON file with bounded reads, temporary-file replacement, Windows backup rollback and recovery, and the same strict codec for settings export and import. Reject oversized imports before reading and ignore stale asynchronous import results.
decisions.rationale: The adapters meet each platform's FR-9.2 storage boundary, while a shared codec makes fresh-profile reproduction and failure atomicity testable across persistence and exchange paths.

decisions.topic: Browser secret location and compensating migration
decisions.decision: Keep the browser AI key in session storage by default; move it to local storage only after explicit opt-in and warning. During a location change, delete the source before writing the destination so no dual copy exists, report partial failures, and compensate by moving the key back when the corresponding profile write fails.
decisions.rationale: The sequence honors the session-first privacy default, avoids transient duplicate secrets, and keeps the visible setting consistent with the actual secret location even when storage operations fail.

decisions.decision: Pin keyring 4.1.4 exactly, expose only load, save, and clear Tauri commands, use service dev.scadmill.app and account ai-api-key, cap secret payloads at 16 KiB of UTF-8, and keep the secret out of every settings, project, and artifact file path.
decisions.rationale: A small explicit IPC boundary implements FR-9.4 and supports file-leak scanning without creating a fallback file-backed credential store. A real OS credential-store smoke remains required before claiming the production round trip.

decisions.topic: Custom themes inside the durable profile
decisions.decision: Validate imported themes against the full Appendix C schema, restrict custom values to opaque six-digit sRGB while Q-0006 is open, apply the conservative contrast oracle, persist the validated tokens with the selected custom preference, and keep operating-system theme preference active until a manual selection overrides it.
decisions.rationale: This delivers the visible C9 half of FR-12.4 with deterministic cross-host validation and live application without claiming owner-final support for the entire CSS color grammar.

decisions.decision: Make the settings profile the runtime authority for rendering, editor, keybinding, theme, and engine-path values, migrate the legacy engine-path configuration only when the profile path is empty, reprobe immediately after path changes, source the initial render quality from the profile, and expose all settings through one searchable keyboard-operable modal and extracted status bar.
decisions.rationale: This preserves accepted A-7/C4 and C6 behavior while removing parallel preference authorities and delivering the FR-9.1 surface without importing later C2, C3, or C5 composition.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-9.2 and AC-9.a immediate application, ordered persistence, restart retention, and rollback
testEvidence.test: A complete profile applies immediately, writes once, reloads in a fresh runtime, restores one section only, rejects invalid replacements without mutation, waits for desktop durability, and rolls back correctly for single and overlapping rejected writes.

red.outcome: RED recorded earlier in this reconstruction: after the 31 C9-owned files were restored but before shared integration, TypeScript reported missing C9 message keys, settings persistence runtime options and commands, the new theme-selection signature, and App/Workbench composition. The exact command start timestamp was not retained, so this is the evidence recording time.

green.outcome: GREEN: 4 files and 27 tests passed, including immediate complete-profile application, fresh-runtime reload, ordered desktop writes, single and overlapping rejection rollback, one-section restore, and exact custom-theme persistence.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-9.3 and AC-9.b bounded secret-free settings export/import
testEvidence.test: UI-exported versioned bytes reproduce every non-secret section and a selected custom theme on a fresh profile; oversized, stale, invalid, and keybinding-colliding imports cause no partial update.

red.command: No contemporaneous original RED transcript was retained for the combined-worktree export/import implementation.
red.outcome: The reconstruction plan records the evidence gap. No retrospective failure is relabeled as the original TDD run.

green.outcome: GREEN within the focused runs: strict codec and SettingsDialog cases passed, including secret-free copy, fresh-profile all-section reproduction, exact custom-theme round trip, oversized rejection before read, stale-result suppression, and keybinding-collision atomicity.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-9.4 browser session-first secret storage and compensating migration
testEvidence.test: The browser adapter uses session storage by default, moves a key only after explicit opt-in, never creates a dual copy during partial migration, reports partial clear, handles blocked storage, enforces the UTF-8 limit, and restores the previous location when durable profile persistence fails.

red.command: No contemporaneous original RED transcript was retained for the combined-worktree secret rollback implementation.
red.outcome: The earlier gate report confirms a genuine secret-rollback RED-to-GREEN cycle but not its exact command output or timestamp; this ledger does not invent them.

green.outcome: GREEN: 4 files and 29 tests passed, including session-first storage, explicit opt-in, no-dual-copy failure paths, partial clear, blocked storage, UTF-8 bounds, secret-safe settings copy, and compensating rollback when profile persistence rejects.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-9.4 and AC-9.c desktop file exclusion and keychain command boundary
testEvidence.test: TypeScript sends AI-secret operations only through keychain IPC; Rust scans every regular file in a representative app-managed settings/project/artifact tree for a disposable sentinel while a separate keychain backend retains it.

red.command: No contemporaneous original RED transcript was retained for the combined-worktree desktop boundary implementation.
red.outcome: This evidence gap is recorded explicitly. The retained implementation never uses a real credential value in ledger or logs.

green.outcome: GREEN: 24 desktop-shell tests passed, including keychain-only round trip through MemoryKeychain, UTF-8 size rejection, settings-file recovery, and recursive absence from representative app-managed files. This is boundary evidence, not a production OS credential-store smoke.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-9.1, FR-12.4, N-3, and N-4 settings and custom-theme presentation
testEvidence.test: The searchable nine-section modal is keyboard reachable, traps and restores focus, remains usable at compact touch sizes, exposes immediate settings and per-section defaults, imports a valid custom theme, and visibly reports persistence or secret-migration failure.

red.outcome: RED recorded earlier in this reconstruction: the restored C9 presentation could not compile until its owned messages, SettingsLauncher composition, real WorkbenchStatusBar, theme selection, and profile-backed App props were connected. The exact start timestamp was not retained.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/theme/custom-theme.test.ts tests/app/App.test.tsx tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx --reporter=verbose; pnpm.cmd exec playwright test tests/e2e/c9-settings-layout.e2e.ts tests/e2e/c0-responsive.e2e.ts --workers=1 --retries=0
green.outcome: GREEN: the focused theme/App/Workbench run passed 3 files and 33 tests; Chromium passed 7 of 7 tests, including modal grid ownership, 320-pixel touch reachability, compact engine chrome, and absence of horizontal body overflow.

testEvidence.criterion: V-1 and V-2 widened C9 regression and policy gates
testEvidence.test: Run the full unit/component, type, lint/source-policy, production build, generated-source, browser, desktop Rust fmt/test/check/clippy, dependency-tree, npm-license, Rust-license, provenance, and diff gates.

red.outcome: KNOWN OPEN POLICY RED: the Rust license scan rejected 19 packages—18 Unicode-3.0 records and unicode-ident as (MIT OR Apache-2.0) AND Unicode-3.0—exactly as Q-0001 records. C9 does not alter policy to hide the result.

green.command: pnpm.cmd test; pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd build; pnpm.cmd check:generated; pnpm.cmd check:licenses npm; cargo fmt --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- --check; cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml; cargo check --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml; cargo clippy --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo tree --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml -p keyring
green.outcome: GREEN except for the separately recorded Q-0001 Rust-license command: 82 files and 594 tests passed; typecheck passed; Biome checked 218 files and source policy passed; Vite built 150 modules; generated parser was unchanged; npm license policy passed 117 packages; Chromium passed 7 tests; desktop fmt, 24 tests, check, and clippy passed; the dependency tree resolved keyring 4.1.4.

questions: Q-0001 remains open: the Rust license-policy command rejects 18 Unicode-3.0 records and one (MIT OR Apache-2.0) AND Unicode-3.0 record. This blocks a fully green PR claim, not C9 product implementation.
Q-0004 remains open only for historical M1 ownership; visible custom-theme import is delivered conservatively under C9.
Q-0006 remains open: custom themes are limited to opaque six-digit sRGB colors so contrast is deterministic; full CSS color grammar support is not claimed.
Q-0011 remains open only for historical M1 ownership; visible settings and rebinding UI plus durable per-user adapters are delivered under C9.

AC-9.c is partially evidenced: recursive app-file scanning and production OsKeychain wiring exist, but automated round trips use MemoryKeychain and mocked TypeScript IPC. A disposable packaged-desktop real-credential save/read/clear smoke remains required.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
